 {"id":"frankenredis-8xmq","title":"Downgrade FEATURE_PARITY.md replication status to honest assessment","description":"# Honest Replication Status

## What
Change FEATURE_PARITY.md "Replication baseline | parity_green" to "parity_gap" or "in_progress" until cross-process replication actually works.

## Why
The current "parity_green" rating for replication is dishonest. The state machine logic works in isolation but no bytes flow between processes. Honesty about project status is a prerequisite for good engineering.

## Implementation
1. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md: change replication row status to "in_progress"
2. Update notes to honestly describe what works (FSM logic, PSYNC decision math, offset tracking) and what does not (actual TCP replication)
3. After replication epic is complete, upgrade back to parity_green with evidence

## No Dependencies
This should be done immediately, before any replication implementation work.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:17.272701785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:52:37.119459043Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:52:37.119284593Z","close_reason":"Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md replication row from parity_green to in_progress with honest assessment of what works (TCP replication, PSYNC, FULLRESYNC) and what does not (runtime REPLICAOF command, replica-of-replica chains, FR-to-FR E2E, diskless replication)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-90iz","title":"Align XREAD BLOCK timeout validation with Redis integer-ms semantics","description":"Redis requires XREAD/XREADGROUP BLOCK to be an integer millisecond timeout and rejects out-of-range values.  {"id":"frankenredis-9vl3","title":"EPIC: Harden security beyond RESP parsing — persistence integrity, connection auth, and adversarial resilience","description":"# Security Hardening Epic

## Why
AGENTS.md Security Doctrine requires: "Defend against malformed protocol frames, replay/order attacks, and persistence tampering." The reality check found:
- RESP parser correctly rejects malformed frames ✓
- CRC64 checksums on RDB files ✓  
- No evidence of replay attack defense beyond basic auth
- No evidence of persistence tampering detection beyond CRC64
- No adversarial fuzzing of the RESP parser or command handlers
- No rate limiting or connection throttling

## What Success Looks Like
- Fuzz test for RESP parser (catches crashes on malformed input)
- Fuzz test for RDB decoder (catches crashes on corrupted files)
- AOF integrity verification (detect truncation/corruption on load)
- Documentation of threat model per AGENTS.md requirements","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.951225550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:21.928344033Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:21.928152231Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: RESP parser fuzz (29c7, 40K inputs), RDB/AOF decoder fuzz (9zk5, 30K inputs), threat model documentation (fap1, 6 subsystems). Zero panics found in 70K total random inputs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-9vl3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-29c7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:07.832736105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-9vl3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-9zk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:07.969048271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-9vl3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-fap1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:08.096146297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
